title: Cost-benefit analysis
description: Practice what you have learned about total utility, marginal utility, total revenue, and costs in this exercise.

assessment_items.question_content:
Phan is deciding whether or not to invest in a project. This project cannot be broken down into increments, and she has the choice of either investing in it or not.

**What is true about Phan’s decision?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: She should examine the total costs and total benefits of the project
She should examine the marginal costs and marginal benefits of the project
It cannot be examined rationally because it cannot be broken down into increments
She should pursue it if the costs outweigh the benefits
She should consider only the explicit costs of the project

assessment_items.question_content:
Le wants to open a Cajun-Vietnamese fusion restaurant. He currently works as a software engineer and earns $\$90{,}000$ per year. He estimates the operating costs for the restaurant, such as the cost of labor and materials, at $\$60{,}000$. He also estimates he will have $\$100{,}000$ in revenues from operating the restaurant.

**Which of the following statements is true based on Le’s estimates?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: He should consider both his foregone earnings and the explicit costs when comparing his revenues to costs.
He should only consider the explicit costs of operating the restaurant when comparing his revenues to costs.
He should only consider his foregone earnings when comparing his revenues to costs.
He should subtract his explicit costs from his foregone earnings when comparing his revenues to his costs.
As long as his revenues are higher than $\$60{,}000$, it is rational to open this restaurant.
The total benefit to producers of selling a good
The total cost of producing a good
The profit a firm gets from selling a good
The happiness that consumers get from consuming a good
The money that a seller gets from selling one more good

assessment_items.question_content:
**What measure reflects all of the benefits to sellers?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Total revenue
Total utility
Marginal utility
Marginal Cost
Total costs

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes an optimal choice?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The choice that maximizes the difference between total benefits and total costs
The choice that maximizes benefits regardless of costs
The choice that minimizes costs regardless of benefits
The choice that equalizes total benefits and total costs
The choice that maximizes total revenues

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the difference between an implicit cost and an explicit cost?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: An implicit cost does not always involve a monetary expense, but an explicit cost does
Opportunity costs can be implicit, but not explicit
Opportunity costs can be explicit, but not implicit
Explicit costs should be ignored when calculating the total economic costs of a decision
Implicit costs should be ignored when calculating the total economic costs of a decision

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes opportunity costs?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The implicit and explicit costs of an action.
The value of all other alternatives
The price of a good
The explicit costs of a choice
The cost of the lowest valued alternative

assessment_items.question_content:
Giang is examining a project that looks at the benefits and costs of cleaning up runoff from a factory that is polluting the river. Unfortunately, he can only look at the costs and benefits on an incremental basis, such as preventing each liter of runoff into the river.

**Which of the following is true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: He should examine at the marginal costs and marginal benefits of each liter of runoff
He should examine the total costs and total benefits of the project
He cannot examine this project rationally because it cannot be looked at as a whole, only incrementally
He should pursue it if the costs outweigh the benefits
He should consider only the explicit costs of the project
